br output format: \"Created bead abc-123\"\n288\t        # or just the bead ID\n289\t        lines = output.strip().split(\"\\n\")\n290\t        for line in lines:\n291\t            if \"Created bead\" in line or \"bead\" in line.lower():\n292\t                # Try to extract ID (format: abc-123)\n293\t                parts = line.split()\n294\t                for part in parts:\n295\t                    if \"-\" in part and len(part) > 5:\n296\t                        return part\n297\t\n298\t        # Fallback: return first word that looks like a bead ID\n299\t        for word in output.split():\n300\t            if \"-\" in word and 3 < len(word) < 20:\n301\t                return word\n302\t\n303\t        # Last resort: try to find a pattern like abc-123 anywhere in output\n304\t        import re\n305\t        # Pattern for bead ID: letters-numbers format like abc-123 (not letters-letters)\n306\t        # This avoids matching words like \"def-in\" or \"some-output\"\n307\t        matches = re.findall(r'\\b[a-z]{2,4}-[0-9]{2,6}\\b', output, re.IGNORECASE)\n308\t        if matches:\n309\t            return min(matches, key=len)\n310\t\n311\t        # Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Captures the full scope of the request\n2. Includes relevant context (project, cluster, specific resources)\n3. Defines clear success criteria\n4. Structures the work for Claude Code execution\n\nOutput ONLY the bead body as markdown. Do not include explanations or meta-commentary.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef load_escalate_prompt() -> str:\n    \"\"\"Load the escalate prompt from the prompts directory.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH, \"r\") as f:\n            content = f.read()\n            # Extract the system prompt section (between \"System Prompt:\" and \"Input Context:\")\n            # For now, just use the whole file - the LLM can handle the structure\n            return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT  # Use default for now\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt not found at {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n        return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateRequest:\n    \"\"\"Request to escalate an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    intent_type: str\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    topic_id: str | None = None\n    context: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"session_id\": self.session_id,\n            \"utterance\": self.utterance,\n            \"intent_type\": self.intent_type,\n            \"project_slug\": self.project_slug,\n            \"topic_id\": self.topic_id,\n            \"context\": self.context,\n            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n        }\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of escalating an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    bead_id: str\n    intent_id: str\n    pending_card: dict\n    status: str = \"pending\"  # pending, created, failed\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"bead_id\": self.bead_id,\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"pending_card\": self.pending_card,\n            \"status\": self.status,\n        }\n\n\nclass EscalateError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for escalate errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass BeadCreationError(EscalateError):\n    \"\"\"Bead creation failed.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate handler for task-profile intents.\n\n    Handles the full escalate flow:\n    1. Formulate bead body via LLM\n    2. Create bead via br CLI\n    3. Return pending-card spec\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    async def formulate_bead_body(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Formulate a bead body using the LLM.\n\n        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n\n        # Build context for the LLM\n        context_parts = [\n            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n\n        # Add any additional context\n        if request.context:\n            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n            for key, value in request.context.items():\n                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n\n        # Build the user message\n        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n            \"\",\n            *context_parts,\n            \"\",\n            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n        ])\n\n        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        # Make LLM call\n        try:\n            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n            return bead_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def create_bead(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead using the br CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build br create command\n        # Using br CLI to create the bead\n        try:\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"br\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n                \"--metadata\", json.dumps(metadata),\n                stdin=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            # Send bead body via stdin\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate(\n                input=bead_body.encode(\"utf-8\")\n            )\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"br CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"br CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n        # Use utterance prefix as title\n        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n        if len(utterance) > 60:\n            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n        else:\n            title = utterance\n\n        # Add project prefix if available\n        if request.project_slug:\n            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n\n        return title\n\n    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from br create output.\"\"\"\n        # br output format: \"Created bead abc-123\"\n        # or just the bead ID\n        lines = output.strip().split(\"\\n\")\n        for line in lines:\n            if \"Created bead\" in line or \"bead\" in line.lower():\n                # Try to extract ID (format: abc-123)\n                parts = line.split()\n                for part in parts:\n                    if \"-\" in part and len(part) > 5:\n                        return part\n\n        # Fallback: return first word that looks like a bead ID\n        for word in output.split():\n            if \"-\" in word and 3 < len(word) < 20:\n                return word\n\n        # Last resort: try to find a pattern like abc-123 anywhere in output\n        import re\n        # Pattern for bead ID: letters-numbers format like abc-123 (not letters-letters)\n        # This avoids matching words like \"def-in\" or \"some-output\"\n        matches = re.findall(r'\\b[a-z]{2,4}-[0-9]{2,6}\\b', output, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if matches:\n            return min(matches, key=len)\n\n        # Last resort: generate a UUID\n        logger.warning(f\"Could not extract bead ID from br output: {output}\")\n        return str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    def build_pending_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_id: str,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n\n        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"pending\",\n            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"bead_type\": \"task\",\n            },\n        }\n\n    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n\n        Full flow:\n        1. Formulate bead body via LLM\n        2. Create bead via br CLI\n        3. Return pending-card spec\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id} to bead\")\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Formulate bead body\n            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n\n            # Step 2: Create bead\n            bead_id = await self.create_bead(request, bead_body)\n\n            # Step 3: Build pending card\n            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id)\n\n            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"dispatched\",\n            )\n\n            # Also update the bead_ref field\n            # (This would require a schema update, for now we track via metadata)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=bead_id,\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=pending_card,\n                status=\"created\",\n            )\n\n        except EscalateError:\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global escalate handler instance\n_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n\n\ndef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n    global _handler\n    if _handler is None:\n        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n    return _handler\n\n\nasync def escalate_intent(request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to escalate an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: The escalate request\n\n    Returns:\n        EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n    \"\"\"\n    handler = get_escalate_handler()\n    return await handler.escalate_intent(request)\n","numLines":420,"startLine":1,"totalLines":420}}}

     Convenience method for simple LLM calls.\n\n        Returns just the text content.\n        \"\"\"\n        request = LLMRequest(\n            system_prompt=system_prompt,\n            user_message=user_message,\n            model=model or self.default_model,\n            max_tokens=max_tokens,\n            temperature=temperature,\n        )\n        response = await self.call(request)\n        return response.content\n\n\n# Global ZAI client instance\n_client: Optional[ZAIClient] = None\n\n\ndef get_zai_client(\n    proxy_url: str = ZAI_PROXY_URL,\n    default_model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,\n    timeout: float = 30.0,\n) -> ZAIClient:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global ZAI client instance.\"\"\"\n    global _client\n    if _client is None:\n        _client = ZAIClient(\n            proxy_url=proxy_url,\n            default_model=default_model,\n            timeout=timeout,\n        )\n    return _client\n","numLines":232,"startLine":1,"totalLines":232}}}

Formulate Bead Body (LLM Call)\n\n**Model:** Claude Sonnet 4 (via ZAI proxy)\n**Timeout:** 30 seconds\n\nGiven the user's intent and available context, produce a well-structured bead body that:\n\n1. **Captures the full scope** of the request\n2. **Includes relevant context** (project, cluster, specific resources)\n3. **Defines clear success criteria**\n4. **Structures the work** for Claude Code execution\n\n**System Prompt:**\n\n```\nYou are ADC's escalate handler. Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Captures the full scope of the request\n2. Includes relevant context (project, cluster, specific resources)\n3. Defines clear success criteria\n4. Structures the work for Claude Code execution\n\nOutput ONLY the bead body as markdown. Do not include explanations or meta-commentary.\n```\n\n**Input Context:**\n- User utterance (verbatim)\n- Intent type\n- Project slug (if applicable)\n- Topic ID (if continuing an existing topic)\n- Additional context (git status, cluster state, etc.)\n\n**Output:**\n\n```markdown\n## Task\n[Clear, actionable description of what needs to be done]\n\n## Context\n[Relevant context about the project, cluster, resources]\n\n## Success Criteria\n[Specific, measurable criteria for completion]\n\n## Implementation Notes\n[Any technical details, constraints, or guidance for Claude Code]\n```\n\n### 2. Create Bead (br CLI)\n\n**Command:**\n\n```bash\nbr create \\\n  --title \"[project-slug] task-verb...\" \\\n  --type task \\\n  --metadata '{\"session_id\":\"...\",\"intent_id\":\"...\",\"origin_surface_id\":\"...\"}'\n```\n\n**Bead metadata includes:**\n- `session_id`: For bead watcher routing\n- `intent_id`: Links bead to originating intent\n- `origin_surface_id`: Which surface originated the request\n- `project_slug`: For project workspace routing\n- `topic_id`: If continuing an existing topic\n\n### 3. Return Pending-Card Spec\n\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"pending\",\n  \"id\": \"pending-{bead_id}\",\n  \"intent_id\": \"{intent_id}\",\n  \"bead_id\": \"{bead_id}\",\n  \"title\": \"[Short title from utterance]\",\n  \"summary\": \"Working on: {utterance preview}\",\n  \"status\": \"pending\",\n  \"urgency\": \"normal|high|critical\",\n  \"created_at\": 1234567890,\n  \"metadata\": {\n    \"project_slug\": \"...\",\n    \"topic_id\": \"...\",\n    \"bead_type\": \"task\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Bead Watcher Integration\n\nOnce created, the bead watcher monitors for bead closure. When the bead is closed:\n\n1. Bead watcher receives the closure event\n2. Reads bead body and extracts completion summary\n3. Routes result to the originating session via SSE\n4. Surface updates from pending-card to result-card\n\n## Routing Decision\n\nThe intent router classifies task-profile intents when:\n- Intent requires multi-step work\n- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\")\n- Intent complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n- Request involves implementation work\n\n**Classification criteria:**\n- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\"\n- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"refactor\", \"optimize\"\n- Time indicators: \"when you get a chance\", \"eventually\"\n\n## Fallback Behavior\n\n- **LLM timeout**: Retry once, then escalate with simplified bead body\n- **br create failure**: Return error to user, suggest manual bead creation\n- **Missing project**: Ask user to specify project context\n\n## Context Sources\n\nFor escalation, include these context fields if available:\n\n- **Git status**: Current branch, uncommitted changes\n- **Cluster state**: Relevant pod/deployment status\n- **CI status**: Recent workflow runs\n- **Existing beads**: Related open beads for the project\n- **Topic context**: Previous turns in this topic\n\nThe escalate strand is the only strand that creates durable work items. All other strands are read-only or transient actions.\n","numLines":152,"startLine":1,"totalLines":152}}}

domains.\n58\t\n59\t## Your Role\n60\t\n61\tGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n62\t1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n63\t2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n64\t3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n65\t4. **Assign** urgency tier\n66\t\n67\t## Output Format\n68\t\n69\tReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n70\t\n71\t```json\n72\t[\n73\t  {\n74\t    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile\",\n75\t    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n76\t    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n77\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n78\t    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n79\t    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n80\t  }\n81\t]\n82\t```\n83\t\n84\t## Intent Types\n85\t\n86\t- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n87\t- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n88\t- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n89\t- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n90\t- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n91\t- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n92\t- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n93\t- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n94\t\n95\t## Task-Profile Classification\n96\t\n97\tRoute to **task-profile** when:\n98\t- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n99\t- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n100\t- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n101\t- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n102\t- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n103\t- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n104\t\n105\tTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n106\t\n107\t## Urgency Tiers\n108\t\n109\t- **critical**: Blocking production, security incident, immediate action required\n110\t- **high**: Important but not blocking, user is actively waiting\n111\t- **normal**: Routine query, no time pressure\n112\t- **low**: Background research, nice-to-have, can be deferred\n113\t\n114\t## Routing Logic\n115\t\n116\tUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n117\t- Look for direct project name matches\n118\t- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n119\t- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n120\t- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n121\t\n122\t## Segmentation Guidelines\n123\t\n124\t- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n125\t- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n126\t- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n127\t\n128\t## Confidence Threshold\n129\t\n130\t- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n131\t- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n132\t- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n133\t\n134\tReturn ONLY the JSON array. No explanations.\"\"\"\n135\t\n136\t\n137\tclass IntentRouter:\n138\t    \"\"\"\n139\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n140\t\n141\t    For task-profile        self.store = store\n147\t        self._zai_client = None\n148\t\n149\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n150\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n151\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n152\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n153\t        return self._zai_client\n154\t\n155\t    async def _get_store(self):\n156\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n157\t        if self.store is None:\n158\t            self.store = get_store()\n159\t        return self.store\n160\t\n161\t    async def classify_utterance(\n162\t        self,\n163\t        utterance: str,\n164\t        session_id: str,\n165\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n166\t        \"\"\"\n167\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n168\t\n169\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n170\t\n171\t        Args:\n172\t            utterance: The user utterance\n173\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n174\t\n175\t        Returns:\n176\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n177\t        \"\"\"\n178\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n179\t\n180\t        # Build user message with session context if available\n181\t        store = await self._get_store()\n182\t        session_context = \"\"\n183\t        try:\n184\t            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n185\t            if session:\n186\t                # Get recent intents for context\n187\t                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n188\t                if recent_intents:\n189\t                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n190\t                    for intent in recent_intents:\n191\t context: {e}\")\n194\t\n195\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n196\t\n197\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n198\t\n199\t        try:\n200\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n201\t                system_prompt=ROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT,\n202\t                user_message=user_message,\n203\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n204\t                max_tokens=2048,\n205\t                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n206\t            )\n207\t\n208\t            # Parse JSON response\n209\t            import json\n210\t\n211\t            intents_data = json.loads(response)\n212\t            classifications = []\n213\t\n214\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n215\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n216\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n217\t                try:\n218\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n219\t                except ValueError:\n220\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n221\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n222\t\n223\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n224\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n225\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n226\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n227\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n228\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n229\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n230\t                )\n231\t                classifications.append(classification)\n232\t\n233\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n234\t            return classifications\n235\t\n236\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n237\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n238\t            # Fallback: return single status intent\n239\t            return [\n240\t                IntentClassification(\n241\t                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n242\t                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n243\t                    confidence=0.5,\n244\t                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting Router - classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\nUses LLM to classify intents by type and project, then routes:\n- task-profile intents → escalate strand (bead creation)\n- other intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..escalate.handler import EscalateRequest, escalate_intent\nfrom ..escalate.llm import get_zai_client, ModelClass\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n\n\n# Router system prompt\nROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT = \"\"\"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp, a universal personal interface that routes user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n\n## Your Role\n\nGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n4. **Assign** urgency tier\n\n## Output Format\n\nReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile\",\n    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\n## Intent Types\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n\n## Task-Profile Classification\n\nRoute to **task-profile** when:\n- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n\nTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n\n## Urgency Tiers\n\n- **critical**: Blocking production, security incident, immediate action required\n- **high**: Important but not blocking, user is actively waiting\n- **normal**: Routine query, no time pressure\n- **low**: Background research, nice-to-have, can be deferred\n\n## Routing Logic\n\nUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n- Look for direct project name matches\n- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n\n## Segmentation Guidelines\n\n- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n\n## Confidence Threshold\n\n- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n\nReturn ONLY the JSON array. No explanations.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=ROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            import json\n\n            intents_data = json.loads(response)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n            return await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent)\n\n        # For other intents, return placeholder for now\n        # TODO: Integrate with fetch + synthesize strands\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"message\": f\"Intent routed to {classification.intent_type.value} strand (not yet implemented)\",\n        }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLE bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n            }\n\n\n# Global router instance\n_router: Optional[IntentRouter] = None\n\n\ndef get_router(store=None) -> IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global intent router instance.\"\"\"\n    global _router\n    if _router is None:\n        _router = IntentRouter(store=store)\n    return _router\n","numLines":382,"startLine":1,"totalLines":382}}}

\"type\": \"error\",\n202\t            \"error\": \"OpenAI API key not configured\"\n203\t        })\n204\t        await websocket.close(code=1011, reason=\"API key missing\")\n205\t \"Returns acknowledgment immediately; results arrive asynchronously. \"\n264\t                \"Use this for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n265\t            ),\n266\t              \"utterance\": {\n271\t                        \"type\": \"string\",\n272\t                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n273\t                    }\n274\t                },\n275\t                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n276\t            }\n277\t        )\n278\t\n279\t        # Register change_voice tool\n280\t        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n281\t            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n282\t\n283\t        voice.register_tool(\n284\t            name=\"change_voice\",\n285\t            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. def route_intent(request: dict):\n330\t    \"\"\"\n331\t    Intent router endpoint.\n332\t\n333\t    Classifies utterances into intent types and routes to appropriate strands.\n334\t    Task-profile def voice_session(websocket: WebSocket):\n    \"\"\"\n    Voice session endpoint using OpenAI Realtime API.\n\n    Handles:\n    - WebRTC connection establishment\n    - Tool-as-trigger store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register audio surface\n    audio_surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"audio\")\n    surface_id = audio_surface_id\n    logger.info(f\"Registered audio surface: {surface_id}\")\n\n    # Load voice prompt\n    voice_prompt = load_voice_prompt(VOICE_PROMPT_PATH)\n\n    # Surface switch handler for audio-to-canvas continuity\n    async def on_surface_switch(surface_type: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle surface switch event (audio -> canvas).\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Surface switch: {surface_type}\")\n        try:\n            result = await handle_surface_switch(surface_type, session_id, voice)\n            # Send acknowledgment to client\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"adc.surface_switch_ack\",\n                \"surface\": surface_type,\n                \"pending_count\": len(result[\"pending_results\"]),\n                \"summary\": result[\"catch_up_summary\"],\n            })\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Surface switch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n    # Create voice session\n    voice = VoiceSession(\n        websocket=websocket,\n        model=DEFAULT_MODEL,\n        api_key=api_key,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        system_message=voice_prompt,\n        voice=DEFAULT_VOICE,\n        logger=logger,\n        on_turn_done=None,  # TODO: add memory extraction\n        on_surface_switch=on_surface_switch,\n    )\n\n    # Start result listener background task\n    listener_task = None\n    try:\n        listener_task = asyncio.create_task(\n            result_listener(session_id, voice, audio_surface_id)\n        )\n\n        # Register dispatch_intent tool\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"dispatch_intent\",\n            description=(\n                \"Dispatch a user utterance to the intent router. \"\n                \"Returns acknowledgment immediately; results arrive asynchronously. \"\n                \"Use this for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n            ),\n            handler=lambda utterance: dispatch_intent(utterance, session_id, voice),\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"utterance\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Register change_voice tool\n        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"change_voice\",\n            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n            handler=handle_voice_change,\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"voice\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Run the session\n        await voice.run()\n\n    except WebSocketDisconnect:\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session disconnected: {session_id}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Voice session error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        try:\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e)\n            })\n        except:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        # Cancel listener task\n        if listener_task:\n            listener_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await listener_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n        # Mark surface disconnected\n        await store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session ended: {session_id}\")\n\n\n@app.post(\"/router\")\nasync def route_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent router endpoint.\n\n    Classifies utterances into intent types and routes to appropriate strands.\n    Task-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads.\n    Other intents are routed to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Routing utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Process each routed intent\n        intent_results = []\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n\n            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n            intent_results.append(result)\n\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"intents\": intent_results,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/escalate\")\nasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async handling.\n\n    For task-profile intents that require tracking and async execution:\n    - Formulates bead body via LLM\n    - Creates bead via br CLI\n    - Returns pending-card spec\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    intent_type = request.get(\"intent_type\", \"task-profile\")\n    project_slug = request.get(\"project_slug\")\n    topic_id = request.get(\"topic_id\")\n    metadata = request.get(\"metadata\", {})\n    context = request.get(\"context\", {})\n\n    logger.info(f\"Escalating intent for session {session_id}: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Create escalate request\n    escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n        intent_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),\n        session_id=session_id,\n        utterance=utterance,\n        intent_type=intent_type,\n        project_slug=project_slug,\n        topic_id=topic_id,\n        context=context,\n        metadata=metadata,\n    )\n\n    # Store the intent\n    store = await get_store()\n    if utterance_id:\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=project_slug,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        escalate_request.intent_id = intent_id\n\n    try:\n        # Escalate to bead\n        result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {escalate_request.intent_id} to bead {result.bead_id}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": result.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n            \"status\": result.status,\n            \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Escalate failed: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/events\")\nasync def canvas_sse(\n    session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n        logger.info(f\"Registered canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent surface from being marked idle.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    if not session_id or not surface_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"Missing session_id or surface_id\"}\n        )\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/topics\")\nasync def get_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get active topic cards for a content={\"error\": \"Topic manager not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n    return {\n        \"topics\": [card.to_dict() for card in Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n        \"component_iteration\": FeedbackType.COMPONENT_ITERATION,\n        \"routing_correction\": FeedbackType.ROUTING_CORRECTION,\n        \"behavior_adjustment\": FeedbackType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTMENT,\n    }\n\n    feedback_type = feedback_type_map.get(\n        request.feedback_type,\n        FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION\n    )\n\n    feedback_req = FeedbackRequest(\n        feedback=request.feedback,\n        feedback_type=feedback_type,\n        context=request.context,\n        session_id=request.session_id,\n        require_approval=request.require_approval\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.process_feedback(feedback_req)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"confidence\": response.confidence,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n        \"artifact_type\": response.artifact_type.value if response.artifact_type else None,\n        \"approval_id\": getattr(response, 'approval_id', None),\n        \"diff\": {\n            \"artifact_name\": response.diff.artifact_name,\n            \"artifact_type\": response.diff.artifact_type.value,\n            \"change_summary\": response.diff.change_summary,\n            \"confidence\": response.diff.confidence,\n            # Don't include full before/after in response to save space\n            # Client can fetch separately if needed\n        } if response.diff else None\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/approve\")\nasync def api_v1_approve_change(request: ApprovalRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve    \"\"\"Iterate a component based on user feedback.\"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/artifacts/{artifact_name}\")\nasync def api_v1_get_artifact(artifact_name: str):\n    \"\"\"Get an artifact's current content.\"\"\"\n    if not _reload_manager:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Hot-reload manager not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    try:\n        # Try as prompt first\n        content = _reload_manager.get_prompt(artifact_name)\n        artifact_type = \"prompt\"\n    except KeyError:\n        try:\n            # Try as config\n            content = _reload_manager.get_config(artifact_name)\n            artifact_type = \"config\"\n        except KeyError:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=404,\n Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Formulate bead body via LLM\n    2. Create bead via br CLI\n    3. Return pending-card spec\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    async def formulate_bead_body(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Formulate a bead body using the LLM.\n\n        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n\n        # Build context for the LLM\n        context_parts = [\n            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n\n        # Add any additional context\n        if request.context:\n            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n            for key, value in request.context.items():\n                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n\n        # Build the user message\n        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n            \"\",\n            *context_parts,\n            \"\",\n            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n        ])\n\n        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        # Make LLM call\n        try:\n            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n            return bead_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def create_bead(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead using the br CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build br create command\n        # Using br CLI to create the bead\n        try:\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"br\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n                \"--metadata\", json.dumps(metadata),\n                stdin=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            # Send bead body via stdin\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate(\n                input=bead_body.encode(\"utf-8\")\n            )\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"br CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"br CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n        # Use utterance prefix as title\n        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n        if len(utterance) > 60:\n            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n        else:\n            title = utterance\n\n        # Add project prefix if available\n        if request.project_slug:\n            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n\n        return title\n\n    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from br create output.\"\"\"\n        # br output format: \"Created bead abc-123\"\n        # or just the bead ID\n        lines = output.strip().split(\"\\n\")\n        for line in lines:\n            if \"Created bead\" in line or \"bead\" in line.lower():\n                # Try to extract ID (format: abc-123)\n                parts = line.split()\n                for part in parts:\n                    if \"-\" in part and len(part) > 5:\n                        return part\n\n        # Fallback: return first word that looks like a bead ID\n        for word in output.split():\n            if \"-\" in word and 3 < len(word) < 20:\n                return word\n\n        # Last resort: try to find a pattern like abc-123 anywhere in output\n        import re\n        # Pattern for bead ID: letters-numbers format like abc-123 (not letters-letters)\n        # This avoids matching words like \"def-in\" or \"some-output\"\n        matches = re.findall(r'\\b[a-z]{2,4}-[0-9]{2,6}\\b', output, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if matches:\n            return min(matches, key=len)\n\n        # Last resort: generate a UUID\n        logger.warning(f\"Could not extract bead ID from br output: {output}\")\n        return str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    def build_pending_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_id: str,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n\n        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"pending\",\n            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"bead_type\": \"task\",\n            },\n        }\n\n    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n\n        Full flow:\n        1. Formulate bead body via LLM\n        2. Create bead via br CLI\n        3. Return pending-card spec\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id} to bead\")\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Formulate bead body\n            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n\n            # Step 2: Create bead\n            bead_id = await self.create_bead(request, bead_body)\n\n            # Step 3: Build pending card\n            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id)\n\n            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"dispatched\",\n            )\n\n            # Also update the bead_ref field\n            # (This would require a schema update, for now we track via metadata)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=bead_id,\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=pending_card,\n                status=\"created\",\n            )\n\n        except EscalateError:\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global escalate handler instance\n_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n\n\ndef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n    global _handler\n    if _handler is None:\n        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n    return _handler\n\n\nasync def escalate_intent(request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to escalate an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: The escalate request\n\n    Returns:\n        EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n    \"\"\"\n    handler = get_escalate_handler()\n    return await handler.escalate_intent(request)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":231,"oldLines":22,"newStart":231,"newLines":30,"lines":[" ","         # br output format: \\\"Created bead abc-123\\\"\\n        # or just the bead ID\\n        lines = output.strip().split(\\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n        for line in lines:\\n            if \\\"Created bead\\\" in line or \\\"bead\\\" in line.lower():\\n                # Try to extract ID (format: abc-123)\\n                parts = line.split()\\n                for part in parts:\\n                    if \\\"-\\\" in part and len(part) > 5:\\n                        return part\\n\\n        # Fallback: return first word that looks like a bead ID\\n        for word in output.split():\\n            if \\\"-\\\" in word and 3 < len(word) < 20:\\n                return word\\n\\n        # Last resort: try to find a pattern like abc-123 anywhere in output\\n        import re\\n        #
